title: Art and Illusion
subtitle: A Study in the Psychology of Pictorial Representation
author: E. H. Gombrich
excerpt: E.H. Gombrich's Art and Illusion is a landmark study of the psychology of pictorial representation. Drawing on art history and perceptual psychology, Gombrich argues that artists do not simply imitate nature but construct images using learned schemata, which are then adjusted through a process of making and matching.
